---
title: Running differential gene expression on UBC subclusters regressing out sex effects
date: 2025-03-31
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To run differential gene expression analysis on the nine UBC clusters identified in Quang's analysis. Similar to the analysis from 2025-03-04, except that sex is included as a latent variable in the differential expression model. MAST is used instead of the default Wilcoxon rank sum test.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/01A_diff_expr.R`. Note that the code was run using the `diff_expr` conda environment (NOT `scrnaseq_env`). This is because differential gene expression was run using MAST (`test.use = "MAST"`) which could not be installed in `scrnaseq_env`.

### Output

The output can be found in `<pailab projects>/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250331/`. There are two subdirectories:

- `regress_sex_only`: here, `FindConservedMarkers` was used with `grouping.var = "dataset"`, `test.use = "MAST"`, and `latent.vars = "sex"`
- `regress_sex_dataset`: here, `FindMarkers` was used with `test.use = "MAST"` and `latent.vars = c("dataset", "sex")`

Each subdirectory contains the following output files and figures:

- differential gene expression results
  - `genes_to_keep.rds`: list of genes that are expressed in at least 1% of cells in (a) the Aldinger dataset, (b) the Sepp dataset, or (c) across both datasets (i.e., intersection of (a) and (b)).
  - `de_genes.tsv`: output table of `FindConservedMarkers` for each UBC cluster (UBC_0 vs. all others, UBC_1 vs. all others, etc.)
  - `cluster_marker_ranking.rds`: top differentially expressed genes in each cluster ranked by log2FC
  - `prop*.rds`: proportion of cells in each UBC cluster by age, dataset, or sex
- plots
  - `avg_expr_heatmap.png`: heatmap showing average expression of top marker genes in each UBC cluster as well as each cluster's proportion of cells by dataset, sex, and age
  - `*volcano.png`: volcano plots showing differentially expressed genes in each UBC cluster


## Results

Overall, the results from both differential gene expression analyses looked relatively similar (i.e., regressing sex only vs regressing sex and dataset). The figures below show the results from regressing both sex and dataset (`regress_sex_dataset/`).

Load packages and functions:

```{r}
#| cache: false
#| cache-lazy: false

library(tidyverse)

# set output directory
out_dir <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250331/"
```

Using the clusters identified by Quang, `FindMarkers` was run on each cluster, resulting in differential gene expression results in each cluster (see volcano plots below). The top upregulated genes in each cluster were then plotted on a heatmap (@fig-marker-heatmap).

![Heatmap showing average expression of top differentially expressed genes in each UBC cluster. Bar plot annotations on top show the proportion of cells in each cluster by age, sex, and dataset.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250331/regress_sex_dataset/avg_expr_heatmap.png){#fig-marker-heatmap}

We can print the actual proportions for each bar plot:

```{r}
walk(
  .x = c("age", "sex", "dataset"),
  .f = \(x) {
    message(sprintf("***Proportion of cells by %s***", x))
    x <- paste0("prop_", x, ".rds")
    prop <- readRDS(file.path(out_dir, "regress_sex_dataset", x))
    print(t(prop))
  }
)
```

One cluster to keep an eye on would be cluster 6 - nearly all of its cells are from male sample(s) in the Sepp dataset.

### Volcano plots

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

# show volcano plots for each UBC cluster
ubc_subclusters <- paste0("UBC_", c(0:8))
walk(
  .x = ubc_subclusters,
  .f = \(clust) {
    cat(
      "\n",
      sprintf("![Volcano plots showing differentially expressed genes for %s in the integrated Aldinger+Sepp datasets. The number of genes tested is shown on the bottom right of each plot, as well as the number of up/downregulated genes (nominal p-value < 0.05).]", clust),
      sprintf("(%s)", file.path(out_dir, "regress_sex_dataset", paste0(clust, "_volcano.png"))),
      sprintf("{#fig-%s-volcano}", clust %>% str_remove("_") %>% str_to_lower()),
      "\n",
      sep = ""
    )
  }
)
```
